title: aglaia roxburghiana
intro: Aglaia roxburghiana, also known as the Roxburgh's aglaia, is a plant species belonging to the family Meliaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Aglaia roxburghiana include its potential to treat various diseases such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Aglaia roxburghiana can be prepared into herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions.
